reasoning: The informal statement says "prove $-(-v) = v$ for every $v \in V$" in the context of a vector space V over a field F (Axler). The Lean encoding sets up V as an `AddCommGroup` which is a `Module` over a `Field F`, which precisely captures "vector space over a field". The implicit binder `{v : V}` is equivalent to universal quantification over v in the statement. The conclusion `-(-v) = v` matches exactly. Although the double-negation identity actually holds in any additive group and does not require the module/field structure, including those typeclass assumptions merely reflects the ambient setting of the exercise (V being a vector space) and does not change the mathematical content of the claim being proved.
